Title: Phevamine A, a small molecule that suppresses plant immune responses
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an amidino group from L-arginine to spermidine to form amidinospermidine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct precursor of prephevamine and phevamine A
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: specialized metabolite; bacterial virulence small molecule
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Suppresses plant immune responses by suppressing spermidine- and arginine-potentiated flg22-induced ROS burst
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for biosynthesis of phevamine pathway intermediates leading to phevamine A production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Clean deletion of hsv operon in Pto with metabolite loss; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; recombinant protein purification; in vitro enzyme assay; LC-HRMS/MS; NMR-supported pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial virulence-associated HrpL induction; in vitro assays with L-arginine and spermidine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hsvA is part of the three-gene hsv operon required for full virulence-associated metabolite production. 2. Recombinant HsvA preferentially uses spermidine as amidino acceptor and produces amidinospermidine from L-arginine. 3. This reaction provides the first dedicated precursor for phevamine A biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene studied mainly as part of operon PSPTO_0873-0875; exact individual locus assignment to hsvA not explicitly mapped in text.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antimicrobial target for disabling bacterial virulence metabolite production without directly targeting plant metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative metabolomics by LC-HRMS; comparative genomics across 292 P. syringae genomes

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses amidinospermidine with L-valine in an ATP-dependent amide bond-forming reaction to generate prephevamine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of phevamine A
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: specialized metabolite; bacterial virulence small molecule
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Suppresses plant immune responses by suppressing spermidine- and arginine-potentiated flg22-induced ROS burst
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes prephevamine formation, an obligate intermediate toward phevamine A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein purification; in vitro enzyme assay with phosphate release and LC-HRMS product detection; heterologous expression and metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Of the two ATP-grasp enzymes in the operon, HsvC specifically catalyzes prephevamine formation. 2. HsvC ligates L-valine to amidinospermidine in an ATP-dependent reaction. 3. This establishes HsvC as the second enzymatic step toward phevamine A biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only HsvC, not HsvB, performed the valine-condensation step in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential anti-virulence target to block prephevamine and phevamine A formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative metabolomics by LC-HRMS; comparative genomics across 292 P. syringae genomes

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Phevamine A; phevamine B under overexpression/in vitro conditions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses prephevamine with L-phenylalanine to form phevamine A and can catalyze a second round of L-phenylalanine addition to form phevamine B in vitro/heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: specialized metabolite; bacterial virulence small molecule
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Suppresses plant immune responses by suppressing spermidine- and arginine-potentiated flg22-induced ROS burst
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces phevamine A from prephevamine and L-phenylalanine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein purification; in vitro enzyme assay; LC-HRMS/MS; NMR of enzymatically synthesized products; heterologous expression metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ATP/Mg2+-dependent in vitro reaction; overexpression conditions in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HsvB catalyzes ligation of L-phenylalanine onto prephevamine to form phevamine A. 2. HsvB can also add a second L-phenylalanine to generate phevamine B under nonphysiological overexpression/in vitro conditions. 3. The data identify HsvB as the terminal assembly enzyme for the physiologically relevant virulence metabolite phevamine A.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phevamine B was not detected from wild-type Pto and is likely not physiologically relevant.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong anti-virulence intervention target because it catalyzes the terminal product-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative metabolomics by LC-HRMS; comparative genomics across 292 P. syringae genomes

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of phevamine A via activation of hsv operon expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream virulence regulator controlling production level of phevamine A
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: RNA polymerase holoenzyme with sigma factor HrpL
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: specialized metabolite; bacterial virulence small molecule
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Suppresses plant immune responses by suppressing spermidine- and arginine-potentiated flg22-induced ROS burst
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phevamine A accumulation by positively regulating hsv expression; hrpL overexpression increased metabolite m/z 434.324 and hrpL deletion reduced it
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative metabolomics in Pto overexpressing hrpL and ΔhrpL strains; prior regulon identification
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Virulence regulon regulator controlling T3SS and hsv genes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Virulence-inducing conditions associated with HrpL activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: hrpL regulates many virulence genes and is not specific to phevamine pathway, but directly relevant because metabolite abundance tracked with hrpL dosage
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential broad anti-virulence target, though pleiotropic due to control of multiple pathogenicity determinants
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic because HrpL also controls T3SS and other virulence factors
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Prior HrpL regulon analysis; comparative metabolomics
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The hsv operon is HrpL regulated. 2. Phevamine A-associated m/z 434.324 increases upon hrpL overexpression and decreases in ΔhrpL. 3. HrpL therefore positively controls phevamine A biosynthesis as an upstream virulence regulator.
